prompt:
We're adding a podcast to the blog, so rssfeed.py needs to grow up a bit. Ticket, in the order I care:

1) Enclosures. An item may carry enclosure={url, type, length}. Render it inside the item as a self-closing tag with attributes in url/length/type order, values XML-escaped, placed after the optional description/categories but before pubDate. Validation: url required and non-empty; type must be MIME-ish, exactly type/subtype with both halves non-empty; length must be a non-negative integer, accepting either an int or a string of digits, rendered exactly as given.

2) Categories. Both the channel and any item may carry categories=[...] (plain strings). Emit one <category> element per entry, escaped, in the given order, right after the description (for the channel: after its description element; for items: after title/link/description, whichever of those exist).

3) validate_feed(channel, items) -> list of error strings, COLLECTED not raised — the publisher UI wants to show all of them at once. Exact message formats (the strings are the API, the dashboard regexes them): 'channel: missing title' / 'channel: missing link' / 'channel: missing description' in that field order first, then per item, 1-based, in input order: 'item N: missing title', then "item N: invalid pubDate '<value>'" for a pubdate that email.utils can't parse as RFC-2822, then enclosure complaints in url/type/length order: 'item N: enclosure missing url', "item N: enclosure type '<t>' is not type/subtype", "item N: enclosure length <val!r> is not a non-negative integer". build_feed() should now run that validation and raise a single ValueError with all messages joined by '; ' when anything is wrong.

Do not disturb the existing output: the golden-file diff means today's documents must come out byte-identical when none of the new fields are used. test_rssfeed.py is the acceptance suite (python3 test_rssfeed.py) — hands off the tests.
